items.title: Unauthenticated File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in. This vulnerability can lead to remote code execution on affected systems.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce (CVE-2025-47110 and others)
items.summary: This update resolves critical and important vulnerabilities, including a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability via server-side template injection (CVE-2025-47110). Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension. This critical flaw, rated 9.8, allows remote code execution via a crafted CacheWarmer cookie.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Security Update Released – APSB26-49
items.summary: This Adobe Security Bulletin addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate security vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Risks include unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption if left unpatched.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store. The attack uses a polyglot to bypass validation, potentially leading to remote code execution and account takeover.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update resolves multiple vulnerabilities (including CVE-2026-21285, CVE-2026-21286, CVE-2026-21296, CVE-2026-21297, CVE-2026-21310, and others) that could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and unauthorized access to store data. Adobe recommends applying patches as quickly as possible.
